Phase-34.NAME: MTProto Integration Stabilization Gates
Phase-34.Goal: Close the end-to-end stabilization gates for verified registration, free MTProto proxy issuance, dashboard UX, admin ops, and production edge readiness.

NonGoals.item-1: No new functional scope beyond stabilization and release readiness.
NonGoals.item-2: No deploy/install redesign outside the approved edge work.

Steps.step-1: Run full backend targeted suite for email, registration, MTProto provisioning, runtime bridge, admin ops, and migrations.
Steps.step-2: Run user frontend and admin frontend builds plus mobile viewport smoke for registration, dashboard, and admin MTProto pages.
Steps.step-3: Run security/redaction review for email tokens, tg://proxy links, derived secrets, admin audit payloads, and logs.
Steps.step-4: Run backup/restore planning update so MTProto assignments and secret rotation metadata are covered by Phase-22 artifacts.
Steps.step-5: Run production-like smoke after krotpn.xyz DNS and wildcard certificate are ready.
Steps.step-6: Prepare release handoff with known risks, rollback notes, and operator acceptance checklist.

Verification.command-1: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_email_delivery.py tests/test_email_verification_guard.py tests/test_verified_registration.py tests/test_phase34_mtproto_integration.py tests/test_mtproto_assignment_registry.py tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py tests/test_mtproto_api.py tests/test_mtproto_admin_ops.py tests/test_db_migrations.py tests/test_scheduler.py -v
Verification.command-2: cd frontend && npm run build && cd .. && node scripts/phase28-register-flow-smoke.mjs && node scripts/phase31-mtproto-cabinet-smoke.mjs
Verification.command-3: cd frontend-admin && npm run build && cd .. && node scripts/phase33-mtproto-admin-smoke.mjs
Verification.command-5: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-34.xml docs/modules/M-035.xml docs/modules/M-040.xml docs/modules/M-041.xml docs/modules/M-042.xml docs/modules/M-043.xml docs/modules/M-044.xml docs/modules/M-045.xml docs/modules/M-046.xml docs/modules/M-047.xml docs/recovery/BACKUP-PHASE-22.xml docs/handoff/PHASE-34-RELEASE-CHECKLIST.xml docs/verification/V-M-035.xml docs/verification/V-M-040.xml docs/verification/V-M-041.xml docs/verification/V-M-042.xml docs/verification/V-M-043.xml docs/verification/V-M-044.xml docs/verification/V-M-045.xml docs/verification/V-M-046.xml docs/verification/V-M-047.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/current-status.xml && mygrace lint
Verification.command-6: operator-live: after DNS and trusted wildcard TLS readiness, smoke https://krotpn.xyz, admin route, API health, one issued MTProto SNI, and rollback readiness.

VerificationMatrix.gate-1: Pending registration sends email without tokens or active access; verified activation is one-time and side-effect safe.
VerificationMatrix.gate-2: Verified activation creates or exposes exactly one free indefinite MTProto assignment, while unverified registration creates none.
VerificationMatrix.gate-3: Runtime bridge applies/replays/revokes policy safely and owner dashboard shows the compact proxy card without breaking VPN actions.
VerificationMatrix.gate-4: Admin MTProto list/detail/health/reissue/revoke remain redacted, audited, and mobile-safe.
VerificationMatrix.gate-5: krotpn.xyz HTTPS redirect, wildcard TLS, shared 443, and issued SNI routing are proven locally where possible and live-smoked only with operator readiness.
VerificationMatrix.gate-6: Phase-22 backup/restore coverage explicitly includes MTProto assignments, rotation marker, SNI, credential mode, and edge DNS/TLS assumptions.

ExpectedExecutionArtifacts.artifact-1: backend/tests/test_phase34_mtproto_integration.py verifies verified-email activation plus MTProto owner issuance/idempotency and unverified no-access invariants.
ExpectedExecutionArtifacts.artifact-2: scripts/phase34-mtproto-stabilization-smoke.mjs verifies static redaction and route wiring across registration, dashboard MTProto, admin MTProto, and edge docs.
ExpectedExecutionArtifacts.artifact-3: docs/handoff/PHASE-34-RELEASE-CHECKLIST.xml records local gates, live-only assumptions, rollback notes, and operator acceptance evidence.

ExecutionEvidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-14: Phase-34 backend targeted suite passed locally with 52 tests across email delivery, verification guards, verified registration, MTProto integration, assignment registry, provisioning, runtime bridge, owner API, admin ops, migrations, and scheduler.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-14: User frontend npm run build passed, then Phase-28 register-flow smoke and Phase-31 MTProto cabinet smoke passed.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-14: Admin frontend npm run build passed, then Phase-33 MTProto admin smoke passed.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-14: Phase-34 static stabilization smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and git diff whitespace check passed.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-14: docs/recovery/BACKUP-PHASE-22.xml and docs/handoff/PHASE-34-RELEASE-CHECKLIST.xml now cover MTProto assignment restore counts, SNI, credential mode, status, and rotation marker without secret exposure.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-6: 2026-05-14: Production-like krotpn.xyz DNS/TLS/shared-443/Telegram client smoke remains blocked-live and must be completed by operator before claiming live readiness.

RedactionMatrix.item-1: Email verification tokens, token hashes, JWTs, refresh tokens, cookies, provider API keys, and SMTP credentials must not appear in logs, smoke output, audit rows, or docs evidence.
RedactionMatrix.item-2: tg://proxy links and derived fake-TLS secrets may appear only in authenticated owner API/UI responses, never in admin payloads or audit records.
RedactionMatrix.item-3: MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX, MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT, certificate private keys, and runtime env values must never appear in frontend bundles, admin surfaces, or smoke logs.

AutonomousExecutionSafety.safe-local: Backend pytest with isolated DB fixtures, frontend builds, static smoke scripts, XML parsing, and MyGRACE lint are safe to execute autonomously.
AutonomousExecutionSafety.requires-operator: Live DNS changes, wildcard certificate issuance, shared 443 production route changes, public MTProto client smoke, and production reissue/revoke actions require explicit operator readiness and rollback window.
AutonomousExecutionSafety.protected-surfaces: deploy/, install.sh, nginx, docker-compose.yml, and live servers remain unchanged unless a later execute packet explicitly scopes them.

AcceptanceCriteria.item-1: Unverified registration cannot create trial, VPN, referral, or MTProto access.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-2: Verified registration creates KrotPN trial and free indefinite Telegram MTProto proxy without duplicate side effects.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-3: Owner dashboard, admin ops, runtime bridge, and local/static edge checks have evidence; production edge smoke remains explicitly blocked-live.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-4: Release handoff clearly states remaining live-only assumptions.

Risks.risk-1: Live Telegram acceptance cannot be fully proven without production wildcard TLS and real client smoke.
Risks.risk-2: Large behavior change in registration requires staged release and rollback clarity.
Risks.risk-3: Phase-28 remains partial-local until V-M-004 billing route/API and V-M-022 VPN compatibility route findings are closed or explicitly waived for Phase-34.
